free_text: Coupled reaction channel analysis of elastic,
inelastic, transfer, and fusion cross sections
for 12C + 208Pb
Bhabha Atomic Research Centre
Bhabha Atomic Research Centre, BARC-TIFR
14UD Pelletron at Mumbai
The targets used were prepared by vacuum evaporation
of Pb-208 (of thicknes approx. 250 micro-gm/cm2)
onto a C-12 backing (of thickness approx.
40 micro-gm/cm2)
Data were digitized from figures
compiled by Gaurav Aggarwal, Sachin Chachra
Sanat Kumar Pandit
Three telescopes (DeltaE-E) of
silicon surface barrier (SSB) detectors were set up
inside a 1 m diameter scattering chamber for measuring
the Projectile-like particles. A monitor (single SSB)
detector was mounted at 20 deg with respect to beam
direction for normalization. A second monitor at 25 deg
was mounted on the wall of the scattering chamber.
The angular distributions were measured in the range
20deg to 173 deg(lab). The angular resolution was about
0.5 deg. The detector thicknesses were typically 20 to
45 micro-metre for DeltaE and 300 to 2000 micro-metre
for E detectors. Proper gain matching in DeltaE and E
signals led to a total energy resolution of
approx. 500 keV.
Digitizing error
Digitizing error
Fig1
Two telescopes (DeltaE-E) of SSB
detectors were used.One monitor (single SSB detector)
was kept at 40 deg for normalization.
Fig4
Two telescopes (DeltaE-E) of SSB
detectors were used.The residue cross sections for
the 2n channel have been measured in the present work
by alpha counting at energies 58.9, 60.9, and 62.9 MeV
to supplement the ER measurement. For ER measurements,
three targets (with thin aluminum catcher foils on the
back) were irradiated at different energies (58.9,
60.9, and 62.9 MeV) for 5 hours each to populate the
compound nucleus 220Ra. After two-neutron evaporation
and a decay of the daughter and grand daughter, the
CN populates PO-210, which has a half-life of 138 d.
The alpha activity of PO-210 has been measured to
determine the 2n evaporation residue (ER) cross
sections. The activity of catcher foil was also taken
into account.
Table-III
Fusion cross section data has been derived by adding
fission cross section with the corresponding
evaporation residues (ERs) at all the energies. ER for
2n was measured in this experiment, others (for 3n, 4n,
and 5n) were taken from lit. (see REL-REF)
